title: Brevo Review 2025: Features, Pricing & Honest Verdict
meta_description: Is Brevo worth it in 2025? We break down pricing, features, pros and cons so you can decide.
h1: Brevo Review 2025: Everything You Need to Know
intro: Brevo is all-in-one crm suite that positions itself for transactional email and multi-channel marketing. Founded in 2012, it has become one of the most recognised platforms in the email marketing space. In this review we cover what it does well, where it falls short, and who should actually use it.
verdict: Brevo is a solid choice for transactional email and multi-channel marketing. Its free tier is genuinely useful. The main trade-off is between its particular strengths and the limitations that make alternatives worth considering for some users.
pros: Good fit for transactional email and multi-channel marketing
cons: Can get expensive at higher subscriber counts
Some features locked to higher tiers
pricing_summary: Brevo offers a free plan, with paid plans from $25/month. Pricing typically scales with subscriber count.
best_for_detail: Brevo works best for transactional email and multi-channel marketing. If you fit that profile, the feature set aligns well. If you need something different, check the alternatives section.

faq.a: Yes, Brevo has a free plan supporting up to 100000 subscribers. This makes it accessible for new senders testing the platform.

faq.q: What is Brevo best used for?
faq.a: Brevo is best for transactional email and multi-channel marketing. Its standout features include email campaigns, SMS, WhatsApp, which suit this use case well.
Brevo is generally beginner-friendly. The interface is straightforward for basic campaigns, though advanced automation features have a learning curve.

faq.q: Does Brevo have an affiliate program?
faq.a: Yes, Brevo runs an affiliate program paying 5 EUR per free signup + 100 EUR per paid. This is worth considering if you plan to recommend the platform to your audience.
